About

I'm a 2B Nanotechnology Engineering student at the University of Waterloo, building hands-on experience across mechanical design, materials testing, and precision fabrication.

My work spans the full engineering cycle: from CAD design and simulation through to physical fabrication, testing, and validation. I care about the details: tolerances, material behaviour, and why something works or doesn't.

Currently expanding into semiconductor physics, SEM, X-ray characterization, and thin-film measurement through Summer 2026 coursework.

I'm actively seeking a co-op position where I can apply these skills in a demanding, real-world engineering environment.

02

Projects

P.01 Sep – Dec 2024 Complete

Triboelectric Nanogenerator

University of Waterloo Design Project

Designed and iteratively optimized a triboelectric nanogenerator (TENG) to maximize electrical output. Led the full CAD design workflow, refining the geometry from an initial 4-arm concept to a final 6-arm configuration to maximize contact surface area and structural reliability.

  • Led SolidWorks CAD design through multiple geometry iterations
  • Prepared and optimized models in Cura for additive manufacturing
  • Evaluated PTFE/aluminum material pairing for triboelectric performance
  • Validated ~110V peak-to-peak electrical output through testing
  • Collaborated in a multidisciplinary team across the full design cycle
SolidWorks Additive Mfg. PTFE/Aluminum Cura Electrical Validation

Prototype · Dimensioned Drawing · Cura Slicer

P.02 Sep – Dec 2025 Complete

Materials Testing & Simulation

University of Waterloo Laboratory Experience

Applied materials characterization, additive manufacturing, and engineering analysis to evaluate the mechanical performance and manufacturability of polymer-based components. Compared simulated FEA results against physical specimen behaviour.

  • Designed dogbone tensile specimens in SolidWorks to ASTM standards
  • Fabricated specimens on Ultimaker 3 using ABS, LDPE, and HDPE
  • Ran nonlinear FEA in SolidWorks Simulation (von Mises stress + displacement)
  • Performed injection molding and hot pressing on 10+ specimens
  • Documented and compared simulated vs. physical specimen results
SolidWorks FEA Tensile Testing Ultimaker 3 Injection Molding Technical Reporting

von Mises Stress · Dogbone Specimen · Displacement

P.03 Sep – Oct 2024 Complete

Python Data Visualization App

Personal Project

Developed a Python application to parse and visualize engineering datasets using bar, line, and scatter plots. Built to support technical analysis and communicate results clearly. A tool for the kind of data-heavy lab work that defines engineering coursework.

  • Implemented bar, line, and scatter plot generation from raw CSV data
  • Designed for reusability across different lab and project datasets
  • Demonstrates software skills transferable to any data-driven engineering role
Python Matplotlib Data Analysis Technical Visualization
PYTHON · MATPLOTLIB
Data Visualization
Bar · Line · Scatter

Upcoming Work

Projects and skills I'm actively developing, work in progress or planned for upcoming terms.

F.01 · IN PROGRESS

Semiconductor Characterization Lab

Summer 2026 coursework covering semiconductor physics, SEM imaging, X-ray diffraction, thin-film measurement, and surface engineering. Hands-on metrology with real devices.

SEM X-ray Analysis Thin-film Nano-fabrication Metrology
F.02 · PLANNED

Advanced CAD Assembly

Planning a multi-component SolidWorks assembly project with full exploded views, motion studies, and manufacturing drawings, demonstrating assembly-level mechanical design capability.

SolidWorks Assembly Motion Study GD&T Drawing Package
F.03 · PLANNED

Extended Materials Study

Expanding tensile testing work to include fatigue analysis, fracture mechanics observation, and multi-material comparisons, building a richer experimental dataset.

Fatigue Testing Fracture Mechanics Multi-material Statistical Analysis
F.04 · PLANNED

MEMS / Nano Device Design

As nanotechnology coursework deepens, planning to design a micro-scale device, building on TENG experience and expanding into MEMS fabrication concepts and nano-scale design constraints.

MEMS Micro-fabrication Nano-scale Design
04

Experience

Sep 2025 – May 2026
Electrical Circuits & Electromagnetism Lab
University of Waterloo · Nanotechnology Engineering
  • Built and analysed analog circuits (resistor networks, RC circuits) from schematics using breadboards, oscilloscopes, and standard lab instrumentation
  • Measured and validated transient circuit response, comparing experimental results against theoretical predictions
  • Applied KCL/KVL to troubleshoot discrepancies between predicted and measured circuit behaviour
Sep 2025 – Dec 2025
Materials Science & Polymer Processing Lab
University of Waterloo · Nanotechnology Engineering
  • Performed injection molding and hot pressing of ABS, LDPE, and HDPE thermoplastics to evaluate manufacturability and mechanical performance
  • Conducted tensile and compression testing on 10+ polymer specimens, collecting and analysed strength, stiffness, ductility, and deformation data
  • Designed standardised test specimens in SolidWorks and fabricated via additive manufacturing
May – Aug 2024 & 2025
Lifeguard / Team Lead
Friday Harbour Resort
